question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to skip block number validation by ensuring that when EARLIEST_AVAILABLE_BLOCK_NUMBER is used, the selected node's lowerEndpoint() is set as the new block number without verifying it against the last processed block number from cutoverService, allowing a gap in the exported block sequence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to export blocks out of order by serving multiple block nodes where node A has blocks [1,100] and node B has blocks [50,200], and by manipulating node activity states to cause the subscriber to switch between nodes mid-sequence, resulting in duplicate or out-of-order records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a resource exhaustion by repeatedly triggering nodesChanged() to return true (by manipulating the discovery service), causing getBlockNodes() to continuously create new ManagedChannel instances without properly waiting for old ones to fully shut down, exhausting connection pool resources and causing block streaming to fail?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: hasBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to export a block from block number 0 (genesis) by serving a block node that reports lowerEndpoint() = 0 when EARLIEST_AVAILABLE_BLOCK_NUMBER is requested, even if the mirror node has already processed blocks beyond genesis, causing the block number sequence to reset and records to be re-exported from the beginning?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to enter an infinite loop of node selection failures by ensuring all block nodes alternate between active and inactive states on each tryReadmit() call, causing getNode() to never find a suitable node and throwing BlockStreamException repeatedly, resulting in a complete halt of record exports?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to silently drop a block by serving a block node that throws an exception during streamBlocks() after the node has been selected but before any block items are received, since the exception propagates up through doGet() without any retry logic for the same block number?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to use a stale node list by exploiting the fact that getBlockNodes() is synchronized but nodes.get() in getNode() is not synchronized, allowing a thread to read the old node list after getBlockNodes() has already updated it, potentially using a closed BlockNode channel?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: hasBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to accept a block range with a negative lowerEndpoint() (other than -1) from a rogue block node, causing nextBlockNumber to be set to a negative value and subsequent block number validation in BlockStreamVerifier.verifyBlockNumber() to fail, resulting in a persistent export failure?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to use a force-readmitted node that immediately fails again, incrementing the error counter and triggering another readmit cycle, creating a rapid error-readmit loop that prevents any valid block from being exported during the loop period?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to export a block with a null node endpoint by serving a block node whose getProperties().getEndpoint() returns an empty string, since blockFile.setNode(blockNodeEndpoint) does not validate the endpoint value before setting it on the block file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to use an incorrect set of block nodes by injecting a new RegisteredNodeChangedEvent that clears the discovery cache, then serving a modified list of block nodes from the database that includes a rogue node, causing the mirror node to stream blocks from the attacker-controlled node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: hasBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to export an incorrect block by serving a block node that reports blockRange.contains(N) as true for block N but then streams block N+1, since hasBlock() only checks the range metadata and does not verify the actual block number in the streamed content before calling streamBlocks()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to permanently skip a specific block by ensuring that all block nodes report a blockRange that excludes exactly that block number, causing getNode() to throw BlockStreamException for that block on every attempt and the block to never appear in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: drainGrpcBuffer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the executor.submit() in drainGrpcBuffer() to silently discard drain tasks by causing the executor's task queue to be full (if the executor is replaced with a bounded queue variant), resulting in gRPC buffer memory leaks that eventually cause OOM and crash the importer, leading to missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to create a BlockNode with an incorrect ManagedChannel by serving a block node whose host resolves to a different IP address than expected, since channelBuilderProvider.get() uses the host string directly without certificate pinning validation, allowing a DNS-spoofing attack to redirect block streaming to a rogue server?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: hasBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to export blocks from a rogue node by serving a block node that reports a blockRange containing the requested block number, while the legitimate node reports an empty range (EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE), causing the rogue node to be selected as the only available source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to use an incorrect block node by manipulating the priority field of BlockNodeProperties, since getNode() iterates the node list in the order returned by getBlockNodes() (which is sorted by priority), and a rogue node with priority=0 would always be tried first?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to export a block with incorrect consensus data by serving a block node that streams a valid-looking block (passing hash chain and signature verification) but with manipulated transaction records, since the block content validation relies entirely on the cryptographic proof provided by the same rogue node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to lose all block nodes simultaneously by triggering a nodesChanged() condition that causes all existing nodes to be closed (currentNodes.forEach(BlockNode::close)) while the new node list fails to be created (e.g., due to an exception in the BlockNode constructor), leaving nodes.set() with an empty list and all subsequent getNode() calls throwing BlockStreamException?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: hasBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to export a block with block number Long.MIN_VALUE by serving a block node that reports lowerEndpoint() = Long.MIN_VALUE when EARLIEST_AVAILABLE_BLOCK_NUMBER is requested, causing nextBlockNumber.set(Long.MIN_VALUE) and subsequent block number parsing/validation to fail with an incorrect block being exported?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to select a node with an empty block range (EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE) by exploiting a race condition where getBlockRange() returns a valid range during hasBlock() check but the node's actual available range changes before streamBlocks() is called, causing streamBlocks() to receive no blocks and the export to be missing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to export blocks with incorrect timestamps by serving a block node that streams blocks with manipulated consensus timestamps, since the timestamp validation in BlockStreamVerifier only checks the hash chain and signature but not the absolute timestamp values against expected ranges?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to create duplicate BlockNode instances for the same endpoint by manipulating the blockNodeDiscoveryService to return a list where the same endpoint appears twice with different properties, since nodesChanged() only checks size and index-based equality, not endpoint uniqueness?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: hasBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to export a block from an incorrect position in the chain by serving a block node that reports a blockRange of [N, N] for a specific block N, causing the mirror node to fetch only that single block and then fail to find any node for block N+1 if no node reports having it?
